query: Kendra wants enough shirts that she only has to do laundry once every two weeks. She wears one shirt to school for each of the five weekdays. Three days a week, she changes into a different shirt for an after-school club. On Saturday, she wears one shirt all day. On Sunday, she wears a different shirt to church than she does for the rest of the day. How many shirts does she need to be able to only do laundry once every two weeks?

nodes.task: Calculate the number of shirts Kendra wears to school in one week. Multiply the number of weekdays by the number of shirts worn each day.
Calculate the number of shirts Kendra wears for after-school clubs in one week. Multiply the number of club days by the number of shirts worn each day.
Calculate the number of shirts Kendra wears on Saturday in one week.
Calculate the number of shirts Kendra wears on Sunday in one week. Add the number of shirts worn to church and the number worn for the rest of the day.
Calculate the total number of shirts Kendra needs for two weeks. Multiply the total number of shirts per week by two.
Calculate the total number of shirts worn during the weekdays. Add the number of school shirts and club shirts.
Calculate the total number of shirts worn on the weekend. Add the number of Saturday shirts and Sunday shirts.
Add the total number of weekday shirts and weekend shirts to get the overall total.
